channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Authorities Investigate Canine Distemper Outbreak Killing 72 Tigers at Chiang Mai Park
item.description: Authorities in Chiang Mai, northern Thailand, are investigating the deaths of 72 tigers at Tiger Kingdom Chiang Mai, a popular tourist attraction where visitors can interact with the animals. The deat...

item.title: Labour's Struggles and the End of Traditional Politics
item.description: Following Labour's third-place finish in the Gorton and Denton by-election, Keir Starmer’s response lacked the self-reflection many had hoped for. Instead of acknowledging Labour’s failure to connect ...

item.title: Honor unveils robot phone with moving camera that can respond to music and gestures
item.description: Honor has introduced a new concept phone featuring a robotic camera that moves in response to music and user interactions. The device, initially teased earlier this year, is set for launch in the seco...

item.title: Severe Winter Storm Hits Northeast United States Causing Travel Bans and School Closures
item.description: An intense winter storm has swept across the northeastern United States, affecting multiple states including New York, Connecticut, and New Jersey. Authorities in New York City have imposed a full tra...

item.title: Analysis of the Causes and Progress of Russia's Invasion of Ukraine
item.description: In 2022, Russian President Vladimir Putin ordered a large-scale invasion of Ukraine, aiming to capture Kyiv, remove its pro-Western government, and reassert Russia's influence over the country. This f...

item.title: Three US soldiers killed and five injured in Iranian retaliation following airstrikes
item.description: The U.S. military has confirmed that three American service personnel were killed, and five others were seriously wounded during an operation targeting Iran, referred to as 'Operation Epic Fury'. The ...

item.title: Sunlight Creates Fiery Optical Illusion at Yosemite’s Horsetail Fall
item.description: A seasonal natural phenomenon at Yosemite National Park in California is once again drawing attention as Horsetail Fall appears to glow like molten lava when illuminated by the setting sun. The striki...

item.title: New Method Uses Gravitational Waves to Improve Measurements of the Universe's Expansion
item.description: Astronomers have long known that the universe is expanding, but the exact rate of expansion, measured by the Hubble constant, remains a mystery. Multiple methods used to calculate this constant have p...

item.title: Rob Jetten Sworn In as Netherlands’ Youngest and First Openly Gay Prime Minister Leading Minority Coalition
item.description: Rob Jetten has been sworn in as the youngest prime minister in the history of the Netherlands and the country’s first openly gay premier. The 38-year-old leader of the Democrats 66 (D66) party secured...

item.title: Security guard fatally stabbed at Milton Keynes shopping centre, suspect arrested
item.description: A 52-year-old security guard, Gary Stanley, tragically died following a stabbing at Centre:MK in Milton Keynes on Saturday at around 17:10 GMT. Thames Valley Police confirmed a murder investigation wa...

item.title: Russian soldiers testify to BBC about commanders executing troops for refusing orders in Ukraine war
item.description: In a BBC documentary titled 'The Zero Line: Inside Russia's War', two Russian soldiers, Ilya and Dima, who have fled the country, described witnessing severe brutality within their own military on the...

item.title: UK Company Director Sentenced for Supplying Aircraft Engine Parts with Forged Safety Documents
item.description: A British company director has been sentenced to four years and eight months in prison after admitting to a major fraud involving aircraft engine parts worth nearly £7 million. Jose Alejandro Zamora Y...

item.title: Police officer killed and two injured in explosion outside Moscow's Savyolovsky Station
item.description: An explosion outside Savyolovsky Station Square in Moscow has left one police officer dead and two others injured, according to Russia's Interior Ministry. The incident occurred shortly after midnight...

item.title: How AI Digital Twins Are Assisting People in Managing Diabetes and Obesity
item.description: As the demand for expensive GLP-1 drugs rises, a Silicon Valley startup, Twin Health, is offering an alternative using AI and wearable devices to help people manage diabetes, obesity, and prediabetes....

item.title: Overview of the Current Situation in the Ukraine-Russia Conflict
item.description: The conflict in Ukraine is approaching its fifth year, with Russian forces gradually expanding control, primarily in eastern regions such as Luhansk and Donetsk (the Donbas), as well as attempting adv...

item.title: The Fascinating Life of Domesticated Silk Moths
item.description: The domesticated silk moth, *Bombyx mori*, is a remarkable example of human influence on nature. Shaped over 5,000 years of selective breeding, these moths have become dependent on humans for survival...

item.title: Aston Martin Faces Major Challenges with New F1 Car and Honda Engine
item.description: Aston Martin entered the 2026 Formula 1 season with high expectations, including the arrival of design legend Adrian Newey, a state-of-the-art factory, a strong sponsorship portfolio, and two-time wor...

item.title: Kelly Osbourne Responds to Online Abuse Following Brit Awards Appearance Amid Personal Struggles
item.description: Kelly Osbourne has spoken out against the cruel online trolls who targeted her appearance after she attended the Brit Awards in Manchester on Saturday night. The 41-year-old star was at the event to c...

item.title: Western U.S. Faces Water Shortages and Increased Wildfires Due to Low Snowpack
item.description: The western United States is grappling with unprecedented low snowpack levels in the winter of 2026, a trend that could exacerbate both ecological and political challenges. Snow accumulation is at les...

item.title: Two Young Men from Dereham Die on Snowdon, Remembered Fondly by Tutors
item.description: Two friends from Dereham, Norfolk, Eddie Hill, 20, and Jayden Long, 19, were found dead on Wales's highest mountain, Yr Wyddfa (Snowdon), after concerns about their welfare were raised. Their bodies w...
